trigger_question: Which SKUs are at risk of stockout next week and why?

root_cause_hypotheses.evidence: PROMO-001 active since W05 — 145% actual uplift vs 120% forecast. Holi in-store display at STR-002 confirmed. Historical Holi uplift 110-140% — this wave is top of range.
North region festive calendar shows elevated demand in W05-W09. Pre-Holi gifting purchases observable in SKU-007 search trends.
Inventory was pre-positioned for 120% uplift but actual demand hit 145%. 25pp gap = 300 units understocked at STR-002 alone.

recommendations.description: Transfer 400 units SKU-001 from STR-008 (Ahmedabad, 3.2 weeks cover) to STR-002 (Delhi, 0.7 weeks cover)
recommendations.service_level_impact: Prevents stockout at STR-002; minor cover reduction at STR-008 (3.2→2.6 weeks — within SL target)

recommendations.description: Expedite 600 units SKU-001 from SUP-A (3-day fast track available)
recommendations.service_level_impact: Closes stockout risk within 3 days; eliminates lost sales risk
recommendations.constraint_check: Cost INR 42,000 > auto-approve; < HITL INR 100,000 — branch manager approval

what_if_simulation.transfer_scenario: STR-008 cover post-transfer: 2.6 weeks (above 2.0 minimum). STR-002 cover post-transfer: 2.1 weeks. Stockout averted.
what_if_simulation.expedite_scenario: Full demand coverage through W09. No lost sales. Incremental cost: INR 42,000.

critique_node.evidence_check: Both recommendations supported by demand data, inventory data, and promo calendar
critique_node.constraint_check: Inter-store transfer: cost within auto-approve threshold. Expedite: correctly flagged for HITL.
flags: RECOMMENDATION: Execute inter-store transfer immediately (auto-approved). Escalate expedite to branch manager.

hitl_outcome.edit: Reduce expedite to 400 units (not 600) — STR-002 inter-store transfer covers 200 units; 400 unit expedite sufficient

trigger_question: Why did SKU-002 demand collapse in East and Central regions? And why is SKU-005 at stockout?

exceptions_raised.note: Demand is normal — supply has been cut

root_cause_hypotheses.evidence: DEL-001: SUP-B confirmed 10-day production line shutdown. 4,200 units SKU-002 + 1,800 units SKU-005 delayed. Revised ETA Feb 27. This is supply-side failure, NOT demand anomaly.
No evidence of demand shift — category sales in East/Central normal. Ruled out.

diagnose_node.key_insight: CRITICAL DISTINCTION: Demand is NOT anomalous — the inventory drop is purely supply-driven. A threshold-only system would misread this as a demand crash. Root-cause node correctly identifies supplier failure.

recommendations.description: Activate SUP-F (QuickSource Alt) for 30% SKU-002 volume — 1,260 units. SUP-F lead time 7 days.
recommendations.service_level_impact: Partial coverage — covers 30% demand through W11. Remaining 70% gap requires cross-region transfer.
recommendations.constraint_check: SUP-F max capacity 30% per SOP — cannot exceed. New supplier activation requires SCM Director approval.

recommendations.description: Transfer 1,800 units SKU-002 from West DC (excess 4.5 weeks cover) to East region stores
recommendations.service_level_impact: Covers 43% of demand gap. Combined with SUP-F: 73% gap closed.
recommendations.constraint_check: Cost INR 38,000 > auto-approve. Requires branch approval.

recommendations.description: No alternative supplier available for SKU-005. Stockout unavoidable unless expedite from SUP-B possible.
recommendations.service_level_impact: SKU-005 stockout expected Feb 19-27 (8 days). Lost sales estimate: INR 216,000. Recommend customer communication and substitution offer.
recommendations.constraint_check: ESCALATE TO SCM DIRECTOR — no mitigation fully closes gap

flags: REC-002C correctly identifies that no action fully closes SKU-005 gap — honest escalation, not false certainty
SUP-F activation requires SCM Director approval per supplier onboarding SOP — correctly flagged
Combined transfer + backup covers 73% of gap — critique validates math

hitl_outcome.edit: None — approved as recommended. Additionally: issue customer advisory on SKU-005 availability.

trigger_question: SKU-003 and SKU-010 demand is spiking in South and West — what is driving this and should we expedite replenishment?

root_cause_hypotheses.evidence: WE-001: South India heatwave 43°C peak. Historical data: cold beverage +65-90% in sustained heat events. SKU-003 (Cold Brew) uplift 80% — consistent with heatwave range.
PROMO-003 Summer Ready Campaign active since W11. 10% discount + in-store cooler placement. Expected uplift 60%.
WE-006: BrewRush Cold Brew -18% price. However, SKU-003 demand is UP not DOWN — competitor impact appears limited or offset by heatwave.

diagnose_node.compound_signal_warning: IMPORTANT: Cannot deconvolve heatwave effect from PROMO-003 effect without MMM. Both signals active simultaneously. Root cause confidence capped at 0.85 — recommendation should note this uncertainty.
diagnose_node.supplier_complication: DEL-003: SUP-C has 6-day delay on SKU-003 raw material. Expediting may hit supply constraint.

recommendations.description: Expedite 1,000 units SKU-003 from SUP-C — request priority production slot; clear alternative bean grade with QA within 48hrs
recommendations.service_level_impact: Prevents stockout if SUP-C can deliver by Mar 9. Risk: QA clearance for alternative bean grade uncertain.
recommendations.constraint_check: Cost INR 124,000 > HITL threshold. Requires SCM Director + QA Director approval.

recommendations.description: Transfer 300 units SKU-003 from North DC (3.8 weeks cover) to South stores as bridge stock
recommendations.service_level_impact: Buys 4-5 days bridge stock. Reduces North cover from 3.8 to 3.1 weeks (within SL target).

recommendations.description: SKU-010 at 1.1 weeks cover — elevated but above stockout threshold. Monitor daily. No action this week.

flags: Compound signal (heatwave+promo) noted — recommendation confidence appropriately limited
REC-003A correctly flags QA clearance uncertainty as a constraint — not a guaranteed fix
REC-003B auto-approve path correct — cost within threshold, impact within SL bounds
LIMITATION FLAGGED: Cannot confirm whether weather signal will sustain 3 more weeks — recommendation valid for short horizon only

hitl_outcome.edit: Expedite 700 units (not 1000) — heatwave forecast shows cooling from Mar 17. QA Director approved alternative bean grade.

trigger_question: SKU-004 West region is showing 4.2x weeks of cover — should we markdown or redistribute?

exceptions_raised.note: Demand drop is 100% — corporate order cancelled, not a natural demand shift

root_cause_hypotheses.evidence: PROMO-004: 2000-unit B2B corporate contract cancelled W12. Pre-positioned inventory at West DC now represents 4.8 weeks cover (SL max: 5.0 weeks). Demand has returned to normal organic baseline (40 units/week/tier-2 store).
Organic demand for SKU-004 flat WoW — not a demand decline. Ruled out.

diagnose_node.supplier_note: DEL-004: SUP-D quality hold on incoming batch is BENEFICIAL — do not expedite SKU-004. Delay helps inventory balance.

recommendations.description: Redistribute 800 units SKU-004 from West DC to East (200u), North (200u), South (200u), Central (200u)
recommendations.service_level_impact: Reduces West cover from 4.8 to 2.8 weeks. Improves cover in East/North/South/Central by 0.4-0.6 weeks.
recommendations.constraint_check: Cost INR 32,000 > auto-approve threshold INR 25,000 — branch manager approval needed

recommendations.description: Apply 20% markdown on SKU-004 West stores for 3 weeks to accelerate sell-through
recommendations.service_level_impact: Estimated sell-through acceleration: 1.5x. Revenue impact: -INR 48,000 over 3 weeks.
recommendations.constraint_check: Markdown requires Finance + Category Manager approval per policy

recommendations.description: Confirm no expediting of DEL-004 SUP-D batch — delay is beneficial given overstock position

flags: REC-004C is a critical non-action recommendation — correctly identified and validated
REC-004A cost is INR 32,000 — above auto-approve but below HITL; correctly routed to branch manager
Compound insight: supplier delay is beneficial — critique validates this counter-intuitive finding

trigger_question: SKU-008 demand appears to have spiked 3x nationally — should we expedite replenishment?

exceptions_raised.note: 3x demand recorded — but cover is 2.2 weeks (normal). Inconsistency flagged.

root_cause_hypotheses.evidence: WE-003: Flash sale on Brand App on Apr 7. POS system recorded 3x demand but inventory position unchanged (2.2 weeks cover). If 3x demand were real, cover would be ~0.7 weeks. Inventory data contradicts demand data — DATA QUALITY ISSUE.
No corroborating evidence — search trends for SKU-008 normal, no social media spike, no weather/event driver. Ruled out.

diagnose_node.key_insight: DATA ANOMALY DETECTED: Demand signal and inventory signal are contradictory. Per routing logic — DATA_ANOMALY route → flag node, do NOT trigger action recommendation.

recommendations.description: Do NOT expedite or transfer inventory. Flag POS data anomaly to IT and analytics team for investigation. Suspend W16 demand signal from forecast model.

recommend_node.key_note: CORRECT ACTION IS INACTION — a threshold-only system would have incorrectly triggered an expedite order costing ~INR 85,000. LangGraph data quality check prevented a false-positive action.

flags: Data anomaly correctly identified via cross-signal contradiction (demand spike + inventory unchanged)
No recommendation to act — correct. False positive prevention is the key value demonstration.
DEMO POINT: Compare to Demo Step 2 (threshold rule) — rule would have triggered stockout alert and expedite
